text.title: 30-Day Onboarding Phase Framework
text.summary: Day 1-30 phase structure with weekly objectives, single-milestone rule, and check-in schedule.

rule.statement: The 30-day phase has one primary objective: build foundation of knowledge and relationships. Do not assign production ownership in the first 30 days; shadow first, contribute second.
rule.rationale: 20% of voluntary turnover happens within the first 45 days. Effective structured onboarding (82% retention improvement per BambooHR) requires deliberate sequencing, not front-loading responsibilities.

list.item: Days 1-7 (Orientation): HR paperwork, IT setup, meet direct team, manager 1:1 on role expectations, review company docs.
Days 8-14 (Foundation): required training modules, shadow key workflows, meet cross-functional stakeholders, primary tools.
Days 15-30 (Deep Dive): understand current projects, review past work, identify gaps, begin small tasks with guidance.

rule.statement: Every 30-day plan must define exactly one meaningful milestone: the first PR shipped, the first sales call completed, the first support ticket resolved. "Completed all training" is not a valid milestone.
rule.rationale: Activity-based milestones measure compliance, not contribution. A contribution milestone creates a shared definition of success and prevents "set and forget" plans that no one revisits.

example.code: # Software Engineer — 30-Day Milestone Ship first PR to production (typo fix, documentation, or small bug fix guided by team lead). PR must pass CI and receive at least one approved review.
# Bad milestone Complete all required LMS training modules by Day 30.
example.note: This measures activity (course clicks), not contribution. A new hire can complete all training and still be lost on Day 31.

rule.statement: Run three structured check-ins during the 30-day phase: Day 7 (first week experience), Day 14 (buddy/navigation), Day 30 (milestone assessment). Each check-in has a written agenda prepared in advance.
rule.rationale: Check-ins without written agendas default to "how's it going?" — which produces no actionable information and misses blocked items that are invisible to managers.

list.item: Day 7: manager 1:1 — first week experience, blockers, questions.
Day 14: buddy check — navigation questions, culture, team dynamics.
Day 30: manager review — milestone assessment, 60-day goals, satisfaction rating.

text.reference: Universal 30-day plan template with week-by-week structure and success criteria.
